entries.question: How do I search for properties?
entries.answer: Use our search tool to filter by location, price range, property type, bedrooms, and more. Save your searches to get notified of new listings that match your criteria.

entries.question: How often are listings updated?
entries.answer: Listings are updated every 15 minutes from the MLS. New listings appear instantly when agents post them. Sold properties are removed within 24 hours.

entries.question: Can I see sold properties?
entries.answer: Yes! View sold properties in any area by using our 'Sold Properties' filter. This helps you understand market values and pricing trends in your desired neighborhood.

entries.question: How do I schedule a showing?
entries.answer: Click 'Schedule Showing' on any listing. Choose your preferred date/time, and an agent will confirm within 2 hours. Virtual tours available for out-of-area buyers.

entries.question: Are listing photos accurate?
entries.answer: Photos are provided by listing agents and verified by our team. We use AI to detect significantly altered images. Report any concerns and we'll investigate.

entries.question: What is the first step in buying a home?
entries.answer: Get pre-approved for a mortgage first. This shows sellers you're serious and helps you know your budget. We can connect you with trusted lenders in our network.

entries.question: How much down payment do I need?
entries.answer: Down payments range from 3-20%. First-time buyers may qualify for 3% down. We recommend 20% to avoid PMI, but many buyers put down 5-10%.

entries.question: What is an earnest money deposit?
entries.answer: Earnest money (1-3% of purchase price) shows you're serious. It's held in escrow and applied to your down payment at closing. Refundable during contingencies.

entries.question: How long does closing take?
entries.answer: Closing typically takes 30-45 days for financed purchases, 14-21 days for cash. Timeline depends on inspections, appraisals, and lender processing.

entries.question: Do I need a home inspection?
entries.answer: Absolutely! Inspections reveal hidden issues and protect your investment. We'll connect you with certified inspectors. Cost: $300-500, well worth the peace of mind.

entries.question: How do I list my property?
entries.answer: Contact us for a free home valuation. We'll handle professional photos, listing creation, marketing, and showings. Most listings go live within 48 hours.

entries.question: What is my home worth?
entries.answer: Use our free home valuation tool for an instant estimate. For accuracy, schedule a no-obligation consultation with one of our experienced listing agents.

entries.question: What are closing costs for sellers?
entries.answer: Seller closing costs are typically 6-8% of sale price, including agent commissions (5-6%), title insurance, escrow fees, and transfer taxes. We provide detailed estimates.

entries.question: How long to sell my home?
entries.answer: Average time varies by market - currently 25-45 days in our service areas. Pricing correctly and staging well can significantly reduce time on market.

entries.question: Should I make repairs before selling?
entries.answer: Major repairs (roof, HVAC, plumbing) should be fixed or disclosed. Minor cosmetic updates often provide good ROI. We'll advise on what's worth fixing.

entries.question: Do you handle rental properties?
entries.answer: Yes! We manage rental listings and property management. Browse available rentals on our site or contact us for property management services.

entries.question: What do I need to rent a property?
entries.answer: You'll need valid ID, proof of income (3x rent), credit check authorization, and references. Application fee: $35-50 per adult. Pets may require additional deposit.

entries.question: Are pets allowed in rentals?
entries.answer: Pet policies vary by property. Use our 'Pet Friendly' filter. Pet deposits typically $200-500. Service animals are always accommodated per fair housing laws.

entries.question: How do I find an agent?
entries.answer: Browse our agent directory by specialty and location. Read reviews, see sold history, and contact directly. We'll also match you with agents based on your needs.

entries.question: What does a buyer's agent do?
entries.answer: Buyer's agents find properties, arrange showings, negotiate offers, coordinate inspections, and guide you through closing. Their commission is typically paid by the seller.

entries.question: How are agents compensated?
entries.answer: Agents earn commission (typically 5-6% of sale price), split between buyer's and seller's agents. Commission is usually paid by the seller at closing.

entries.question: Can I work with multiple agents?
entries.answer: We recommend committing to one agent for loyalty and focused service. However, you can work with different agents for buying vs selling properties.

entries.question: How do I become an agent?
entries.answer: Contact our brokerage about joining HomeFind Realty. We offer competitive splits, training, lead generation, and marketing support. Must have active real estate license.

entries.question: Are your agents licensed?
entries.answer: All our agents are licensed real estate professionals in their respective states. License numbers are displayed on agent profiles. Verify through your state's real estate commission.

entries.question: What is MLS?
entries.answer: MLS (Multiple Listing Service) is a database of properties for sale by member brokers. It's the most comprehensive source of listings. We have full MLS access.
